nodes.label: AI as comprehensive search tool
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I would really describe it as a collaboration, more a more advanced search/indexing

nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.grounding_span: Rather than wait for inspiration to come to me, I can make my creative process move faster

nodes.label: AI as tool for visual innovation in pitch decks
nodes.grounding_span: using AI tools to create proof-of-concepts for pitches

nodes.label: AI as inevitable industry disruptor
nodes.label_negative: AI creating homogeneous baseline and undervaluing originality
nodes.grounding_span: AI's presence in writing will create a more homogenous and good/okay base playing field

nodes.label: Creative productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: Rather than wait for inspiration to come to me, I can make my creative process move faster

nodes.label: Creative control and originality
nodes.grounding_span: I am the sole driver of creative decisions

nodes.label: Originality and groundbreaking storytelling
nodes.grounding_span: the importance of originality would likely be downplayed

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I would really describe it as a collaboration, more a more advanced search/indexing

nodes.label: Concern about AI devaluing writer compensation and originality
nodes.grounding_span: stiffing them on the money they would have received

nodes.label: Optimistic anticipation of AI for pitching
nodes.grounding_span: I can totally see myself doing a work-in-progress AI generated short/scenes/sequence as a way to sell a project

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: it will send me on a path towards what I actually want by giving some examples and eliminating what I don't want

nodes.label: Active targeted seeking over passive waiting
nodes.grounding_span: Rather than wait for inspiration to come to me, I can make my creative process move faster by trying to be more active/targeted
